reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully captures Rudin's exercise. `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin k)` is the standard formalization of ℝ^k. The hypothesis `¬ Set.Countable E` encodes "E is uncountable". The definition `P = {x | ∀ U ∈ 𝓝 x, ¬ Set.Countable (U ∩ E)}` correctly defines condensation points: since `U ∈ 𝓝 x` is equivalent to "U contains an open neighborhood of x", this quantification is equivalent to "every open neighborhood of x has uncountable intersection with E", which is the standard definition. The conclusion `Set.Countable (E \ P)` precisely states that at most countably many points of E are not condensation points.
